entries.title: Reactor online. Initializing API changelog.
entries.description: We’ll be posting changes to the API starting from here. You can always find the current state of our API at https://unsplash.com/documentation

entries.title: Existing deprecations
entries.description: Some endpoints and behaviour have already been deprecated, and are no longer officially supported:

entries.title: Updated API Guidelines
entries.description: We’ve updated the official Unsplash API Guidelines to include two new technical guidelines for all API applications:

entries.title: Filter photo search results by orientation
entries.description: The /search and /search/photos/ endpoints will now accept an orientation parameter, and return photos only matching that. Valid values are landscape , portrait , and squarish .

entries.title: Instagram username now public
entries.description: Both full and abbreviated user responses now include the instagram_username attribute without requiring the read_user scope.

entries.title: Categories EOL
entries.description: Categories endpoints won’t yet 404, but are returning empty data structures. e.g., /categories/:id/photos => []

entries.title: Abbreviation of current_user_collections
entries.description: For performance reasons we are removing some of the deeply-nested objects returned in the current_user_collections photo attribute. current_user_collections returns a list of collection objects, which will no longer include the user or cover_photo information.

entries.title: Curated Collections deprecation
entries.description: Curated Collections are now deprecated. This includes endpoints:

entries.title: Categories removal
entries.description: Categories endpoints have been removed.

entries.title: Abbreviation of top photos in users search and user endpoints
entries.description: For performance reasons we are removing some of the deeply-nested photo objects returned in the search/users and users/:username endpoints.

entries.title: API parameter resizing deprecation
entries.description: The custom cropping and resizing parameters accepted by the get a photo , get a random photo , and get a user endpoints are being deprecated and replaced instead with dynamic image URLs which are capable of more resizing and cropping options.

entries.title: Curated Collections removal
entries.description: The deprecated Curated Collection endpoints have been removed. This includes:

entries.title: Deprecated endpoint EOL: curated photos, old search, photo stats
entries.description: Three deprecated endpoints have been removed:

entries.title: New filtering and ordering options
entries.description: GET /search/photos adds support for orientation, dominant color filtering, and relevancy vs latest ordering:

entries.title: Content filtering
entries.description: We’ve added a new section to the API documentation to publicly clarify our content safety guidelines :

entries.title: Deprecation of ordering by ‘popular’
entries.description: We are deprecating the option to order photos by popular on all endpoints (e.g., GET /photos ). While the functionality will still exist in the immediate future, it will be soon be removed.

entries.title: User likes statistics deprecation
entries.description: The ‘likes’ data in the /users/:username/statistics response has been deprecated. The total value will be returning 0 going forward, with the entire fragment (i.e., including historical info) to be removed in the near future.

entries.title: Dynamic Client Registration Support Added
entries.description: For decentralized applications, like open source CMSs WordPress and Ghost, we’ve added support for dynamic client registration, which allows developers to grant individual API keys to users with a user-friendly signup process.

entries.title: Support for languages beta
entries.description: We are adding support for non-english input languages to the search endpoint.

entries.title: Collections ID change and featured deprecation
entries.description: Two changes to the Collections API:

entries.title: Added topic endpoints
entries.description: We have added new endpoints for topics:

entries.title: BlurHash added to photo objects
entries.description: All photo objects from the Unsplash API now include a blur_hash string. This is a compact representation of an image which can be used as a placeholder before the real image loads.

entries.title: Query string added to photo download_location URL
entries.description: Query string parameters have been added to the photo’s download_location URL. This may cause issues for client implementations adding their access token to the URL via string concatenation.

entries.title: GET /collections/featured removal
entries.description: Following up on its deprecation in September, GET /collections/featured has been removed.

entries.title: Tags property from search endpoints deprecation
entries.description: The tags property from the /search and /search/photos endpoint is deprecated and will eventually be removed. This was an undocumented field and is superseded by tags_preview

entries.title: Unsplash Source being deprecated
entries.description: Unsplash Source is being deprecated. Existing uses will continue to work, however for new projects use the full Unsplash API .

entries.title: Unsplash Source sunset
entries.description: Unsplash Source has been officially unsupported since its deprecation in 2021. As part of the final sunsetting, we will first wind down by disabling the search feature, and in the coming weeks turn off the application entirely.

entries.title: Removal of order_by from GET /photos
entries.description: The photos Editorial feed will no longer support the order_by parameter, instead only returning photos in the latest order (the existing default).

entries.title: Illustration access enabled for all API partners
entries.description: All API partners can now access illustrations. Previously, this required a specific permission. Endpoints that return photos will now include illustrations as well.
